title: Student Activities (Local Policy)
description: This policy governs extracurricular and school-sponsored student activities, clubs, and groups within Conroe ISD, and is being updated to include new rules around student participation in these organizations. The updates would require written parental consent before a student can join certain student groups, among other new provisions. This matters to parents because it gives them a more direct role in deciding which organizations and activities their child participates in at school.

timeline_local.summary: Policy FM (Local) for Conroe ISD is being updated to add three new sections: (1) Student Groups — requiring parental written consent before a student participates in any district-authorized or sponsored student group; (2) Prohibitions — prohibiting the district from authorizing or sponsoring student groups based on sexual orientation or gender identity (as defined in EMB Local), and prohibiting student groups from violating topic restrictions in Board Policies EFA, EFB, EMB, and EMI (all Local); and (3) Holidays or Commemorative Months — establishing a three-prong test for how clubs may acknowledge federal or state holidays or commemorative months, requiring acknowledgment of significance, connection to history/themes of the state and nation, and consistency with TEKS.
changes_added: Student Groups: District may authorize or sponsor a student group. Before a student may participate in a student group, the parent of or person standing in parental relation to a student enrolled in the District shall provide written consent for participation.
Prohibitions: The District shall not authorize or sponsor a student group based on sexual orientation or gender identity as defined in EMB (Local).
Student groups shall not violate the prohibition on certain topics found in Board Policies EFA (Local), EFB (Local), EMB (Local) and EMI (Local).

Holidays or Commemorative Months: If the club wants to acknowledge the significance of a federal or state holiday or commemorative month, consistent with Education Code 11.005(e)(2) and EMB (Local), the club acknowledgment should generally meet a three-prong test:

1. It should acknowledge the significance of a federal or state holiday or commemorative month (established through a Presidential Proclamation, Congressional law or Resolution, Gubernatorial Proclamation, or Texas legislative law or Resolution);
2. It should acknowledge how the holiday or month fits into the themes of history and the stories of this state and the United States of America; and
3. It should be consistent with the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills (TEKS).
timeline_local.discussion: policy EM (teaching about controversial issues). Raised two specific concerns: (1) the addition of "unpatriotic or anti-American sentiments" as prohibited — terms he called undefined and ambiguous; (2) a new "prohibited terms" section banning "Latinx" and "minor attracted person," which he opposed. He urged the board to give these revisions appropriate scrutiny.
timeline_local.notes: The added text appears in red/underlined format indicating new language. The existing sections (Extracurricular Activity Absences, Use of District Facilities, School Insignia, Participation in UIL Activities) appear to be unchanged. This is Policy Update Page Number 20 for Conroe ISD. No strikethrough/removed text is visible on this page.

description_local: This policy governs student participation in school clubs, organizations, extracurricular activities, and other school-sponsored groups. It applies to all students in the district, and in some cases directly involves parents, such as when written consent is required before a student can join certain groups. This policy matters to parents and community members because it shapes what activities are available to students, sets expectations for participation, and gives parents a say in some of the groups their children become involved in at school.
